html:
[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 芦席覆一尸者 - Literally means 'a corpse covered with reed mats,' used to describe a corpse lying on a bed of reed mats.
  • 募钱收敛 - Means 'to raise money for burial,' indicating the act of collecting money for the funeral.
  • 愈矣，愈矣 - Means 'I am better, I am better,' a phrase used by the resurrected person to indicate recovery.
  • 卷芦席，向众叩头谢 - Means 'rolled up the reed mats and bowed to the crowd in gratitude,' describing the action of the resurrected person.
  • 共荷钱而去 - Means 'carried the money away,' indicating the act of taking the collected money.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金陵城中 - Refers to the city of Jinling (present-day Nanjing) during the Southern Dynasties period.
  • 芦席 - Refers to reed mats, a common material used for bedding and covering in ancient China.
  • 常州 - Refers to Changzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 节妇 - Refers to a chaste widow, a term used to describe a woman who remains faithful to her deceased husband.
  • 鬼神 - Refers to spirits or supernatural beings, a concept deeply rooted in Chinese culture and religion.

Proper Nouns

  • 金陵城中 - The city of Jinling (present-day Nanjing).
  • 常州 - The city of Changzhou.
  • 汪氏子 - The merchant named Wang.
  • 金姓者 - The friend named Jin.
  • 虎丘山 - Tiger Hill, a famous hill in Suzhou.
  • 望山桥 - A bridge in Suzhou.
  • 汪节妇 - Mrs. Wang, the chaste widow.
  • 鬼神 - Spirits or supernatural beings.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 弱冠 - Refers to a man at the age of 20, marking the transition from youth to adulthood.
  • 割臂之盟 - An archaic expression meaning 'a blood oath,' symbolizing a deep and binding promise.
  • 雉经 - Refers to the act of hanging oneself, often associated with extreme grief or despair.
  • 仰药自尽 - Means 'to take poison and commit suicide,' another method of self-destruction.
  • 俗有六冲之忌 - Refers to a traditional belief that certain ages, including the sixth year, are considered unlucky for marriage.
  • 拘牵俗忌 - Means 'to be bound by superstitious taboos,' implying that societal beliefs led to tragic consequences.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 浙右 - Refers to the region of Zhejiang, historically significant in Chinese culture and literature.
  • 庠序 - Refers to ancient schools or educational institutions, symbolizing a man of learning.
  • 早丧父母 - Indicates the protagonist's tragic background of losing his parents at a young age.
  • 媒妁 - Refers to matchmakers, traditional figures in Chinese marriage customs.
  • 雉经死 - A specific act of suicide, often associated with women in ancient Chinese literature, symbolizing extreme grief or loyalty.
  • 仰药自尽 - Another method of suicide, often used in literature to depict the protagonist's despair.

Proper Nouns

  • 龚某 - The name of a man, but his specific identity is unknown.
  • 京兆试 - Refers to the provincial examination, a key part of the imperial examination system.
  • 部郎 - A title for a mid-level official in the imperial bureaucracy.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 扬州东乡十里田 - Ten li east of Yangzhou
  • 看青苗人 - Someone who watches over young crops
  • 野宿棚内 - Sleeping in a shelter in the wild
  • 二里外河边土地庙前有火光 - A firelight two miles away at the riverbank in front of a land temple
  • 或大或小，忽明忽暗 - Fluctuating in size and brightness
  • 席地坐 - Sitting on the ground
  • 旁置土锉，支以石，聚薪燃之 - A tripod placed on stones and fuel gathered and lit
  • 朝南海还 - Returning from the South Sea
  • 腹饥 - Hungry
  • 煮食耳 - Just cooking food
  • 和尚得毋食肉 - Are you eating meat?
  • 戏揭其盖 - Playfully lifting the lid
  • 所煮者两婴儿胎也 - What the monk is cooking is the fetuses of two infants
  • 凡吾曹炼金刚禅者 - A group of practitioners of the 'Diamond Sutra' meditation
  • 须食男胎三十六个 - Need to consume 36 male fetuses to achieve enlightenment
  • 吾止食其七 - I have only consumed seven
  • 此吾福薄也 - This is because my fortune is thin
  • 速毙吾，无多言 - Kill me quickly, don't waste words
  • 击杀之 - They killed him
  • 投其尸于河 - They threw his body into the river

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 扬州 - Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, China
  • 土地庙 - Land temple, a local temple dedicated to the land god, often found in rural areas
  • 南海 - South Sea, which could be the South China Sea or the Indian Ocean, depending on the context
  • 金刚禅 - Diamond Sutra meditation, a form of Buddhist meditation
  • 男胎 - Male fetuses, considered in this context as a form of offering or sacrifice in certain esoteric practices

Proper Nouns

  • 扬州 - Yangzhou
  • 南海 - South Sea
  • 金刚禅 - Diamond Sutra meditation

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 行乞于市 - Means 'to beg in the market,' a common activity of beggars.
  • 多历年所 - Means 'for many years,' indicating a long period of time.
  • 人皆识之 - Means 'everyone knows him,' emphasizing the familiarity of the character.
  • 因幼子病危 - Means 'because his young son is gravely ill,' explaining the urgency of the situation.
  • 自朝至日昃 - Means 'from morning until noon,' indicating a long duration of time.
  • 不遑暇食 - Means 'too busy to have time for food,' highlighting the intensity of the situation.
  • 焉有食食汝乎 - Means 'how can we feed you,' a rhetorical question expressing the impossibility of feeding the beggar.
  • 出言曰 - Means 'spoke and said,' indicating a sudden change in the beggar's condition.
  • 何不谋之于我 - Means 'why not consult me,' suggesting the beggar's confidence in his abilities.
  • 我虽生而哑 - Means 'although I was born mute,' explaining the beggar's condition.
  • 然自幼识字 - Means 'but since childhood, I could read,' indicating his education despite his disability.
  • 喜观医书 - Means 'I enjoy reading medical books,' showing his interest in medicine.
  • 故颇知医 - Means 'thus I know a lot about medicine,' explaining his medical knowledge.
  • 父殁家贫 - Means 'after my father's death, our family became poor,' explaining the economic hardship.
  • 又以不能言 - Means 'and because I cannot speak,' further explaining the difficulty.
  • 有术无可施 - Means 'there was no way to apply my skills,' indicating the frustration.
  • 故流而为丐耳 - Means 'thus I became a beggar,' explaining the final outcome.
  • 何以能言 - Means 'why can I speak now,' a question about the change in his condition.
  • 则亦不自知也 - Means 'but I don't know why,' indicating uncertainty.
  • 医名大噪 - Means 'his medical reputation became very famous,' highlighting the success.
  • 县壶城隍庙 - Means 'he set up a clinic at the Temple of the City God,' indicating his new role.
  • 就诊者日以千百计 - Means 'thousands of patients came to see him every day,' emphasizing the demand.
  • 有杭州黄质文路过无锡 - Means 'a man named Huang Zhiwen from Hangzhou passed through Wuxi,' introducing a new character.
  • 访之 - Means 'visited him,' indicating the visit.
  • 见其所开脉案不成文理 - Means 'saw that the prescriptions he wrote were not coherent,' criticizing his work.
  • 所书药名亦多别字 - Means 'the names of the medicines he wrote were often misspelled,' further criticizing his work.
  • 恐其术止一时偶中 - Means 'I fear his skills are just a lucky coincidence,' expressing doubt.
  • 竟以为高手医 - Means 'and yet he is considered a great doctor,' highlighting the contradiction.
  • 吾不信也 - Means 'I do not believe it,' expressing disbelief.
  • 或曰 - Means 'someone said,' introducing a comment.
  • 哑子能言，亦大奇事 - Means 'a mute speaking is also a great wonder,' expressing amazement.
  • 安知其始非伪为哑以行乞欤 - Means 'how can we know if he was pretending to be mute to beg,' expressing skepticism.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 无锡 - Refers to Wuxi, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 阍人 - Refers to a gatekeeper or servant.
  • 主人 - Refers to the master of the house.
  • 幼子病危 - Refers to a young son who is gravely ill.
  • 日昃 - Refers to noon, a time when the sun is at its highest point.
  • 不遑暇食 - Refers to being too busy to eat.
  • 食食汝乎 - Refers to feeding the beggar.
  • 脉案 - Refers to medical prescriptions or diagnoses.
  • 文理 - Refers to coherence or logical structure.
  • 别字 - Refers to misspellings or incorrect characters.
  • 高手医 - Refers to a highly skilled doctor.
  • 杭州 - Refers to Hangzhou, a city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 黄质文 - Refers to a man named Huang Zhiwen.
  • 城隍庙 - Refers to the Temple of the City God, a place of worship and sometimes a clinic.
  • 就诊者 - Refers to patients or those seeking medical treatment.
  • 岁之冬 - Refers to the winter season.
  • 伪为哑 - Refers to pretending to be mute.
  • 行乞 - Refers to begging for a living.

Proper Nouns

  • 无锡 - Wuxi, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 阍人 - The gatekeeper or servant.
  • 主人 - The master of the house.
  • 幼子 - The young son who is gravely ill.
  • 日昃 - Noon.
  • 黄质文 - A man named Huang Zhiwen from Hangzhou.
  • 城隍庙 - The Temple of the City God.
  • 就诊者 - Patients or those seeking medical treatment.
  • 岁之冬 - The winter season.

[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 同年中式 - Refers to people who passed the examination in the same year, and '中式' means 'to pass the examination.'
  • 轟然俱出 - Describes the sudden and loud departure of the people.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道光 - The reign title of Emperor Daoguang of the Qing Dynasty.
  • 丁酉 - A sexagenary cycle term, indicating a specific year in the lunar calendar.
  • 鄉試 - The provincial examination, one of the three levels of the imperial examination system.
  • 鬼神 - Refers to supernatural beings, often used in classical Chinese to explain unexplained phenomena.

Proper Nouns

  • 沈涛 - The name of the person who had the dream.
  • 沈響泉 - The name of the person who actually passed the examination.
  • 湖州 - The place name Huzhou, a city in Zhejiang Province.
  • 雙林鎮 - The town name Shuanglin, located in Huzhou.

[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 扬州之北鄙雷塘乡 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the northern frontier of Yangzhou, in Leitang Village.' '北鄙' is a classical term for a frontier or border region.
  • 物女 - Rare and obscure term, possibly referring to a mythical or supernatural female figure. Appears in the *Spring and Autumn Annals* (*春秋繁露*) as '干溪有物女, 水尽则女见,' suggesting a creature that appears when conditions are right (e.g., when water dries up).
  • 交辄病，病重者死 - Literal phrase meaning 'those who engage with her fall ill, and those who become severely ill die.'
  • 日加午 - Time expression meaning 'at midday' or 'when the sun is at its zenith.'
  • 雷火从地出 - Vivid description of 'lightning fire erupting from the ground.'
  • 如猪而无尾，如牛而无角 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'like a pig without a tail, like a cow without horns,' referring to the creature's unusual appearance.
  • 周身白毛，两背至胁有黑毛成如意形 - Detailed description of the creature's fur: 'white hair all over the body, with black hair forming a wish-fulfilling pattern on the back and sides.'
  • 腹下一肉条长二尺余 - Description of 'a fleshy strip on the belly, two feet long.'
  • 不可向迩 - Phrase meaning 'unbearable to approach' or 'unpleasant to be near.'
  • 称之重二百余斤 - Description of 'weighing over two hundred pounds.'
  • 此螭虎也 - Conclusion identifying the creature as 'a chīhǔ,' a mythical beast often depicted as a dragon or serpent.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 扬州 - Historical region in China, known for its cultural and economic significance.
  • 雷塘乡 - Specific village in Yangzhou, where the event took place.
  • 隋炀帝 - Emperor Yang of Sui, a controversial ruler of the Sui Dynasty, known for his ambitious construction projects and military campaigns.
  • 观音庵 - Buddhist temple dedicated to Guanyin, the bodhisattva of compassion.
  • 老僧 - Old monk, likely a respected figure in the local community.
  • 螭虎 - Mythical creature, often associated with water and danger. In Chinese mythology, it is sometimes depicted as a dragon or serpent.

Proper Nouns

  • 扬州 - Historical region in China.
  • 雷塘乡 - Specific village in Yangzhou.
  • 隋炀帝 - Emperor Yang of Sui.
  • 观音庵 - Buddhist temple.
  • 老僧 - Old monk.
  • 螭虎 - Mythical creature.

[214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鄉人脔割而焚之，臭聞里外 - Vivid description of a violent act where locals dismembered and burned someone, with the smell spreading throughout the neighborhood.
  • 愚按《说文》，螭若龙而黄 - Scholarly reference to the *Shuowen Jiezi* dictionary, which describes a *chi* (a mythical creature) as resembling a dragon but being yellow.
  • 此物白毛黑文而无鳞，未必其为螭也 - Skeptical remark that the creature in question, with white fur and black markings but no scales, might not be a *chi*.
  • 京师十刹海烟袋斜街，有山西刘姓之女，小字香珠 - Detailed location and personal background, mentioning a girl named Xiangzhu from Shanxi who lived in a specific area of Beijing.
  • 自幼许嫁同乡黄某 - Indicates that she was betrothed to a local man named Huang from a young age.
  • 黄学贾于外，久无音问 - Explains that Huang went to learn trade elsewhere and lost contact.
  • 刘姓中落，女遂流落烟花 - Describes how the Liu family fell on hard times, leading the girl to become involved in the entertainment industry.
  • 已而黄某积累得数百金，挟之至都 - Narrates Huang's success in accumulating wealth and returning to the capital with it.
  • 将求故剑，舍于西河沿逆旅 - Indicates Huang's intention to find his former betrothed and his temporary stay at an inn near Xihéyán.
  • 主人利其腰缠，诱与冶游 - Describes the innkeeper's greed and how he lured Huang into a life of debauchery.
  • 黄年少，堕其计 - Explains that Huang, being young, fell for the innkeeper's scheme.
  • 妓有秀兰者，与黄极相得 - Introduces a courtesan named Xiulan who became very close to Huang.
  • 无何，黄之金尽，妓家将有逐客之令 - Narrates how Huang spent all his money, and the courtesan's household planned to expel him.
  • 秀兰乃托言赴庙会，与黄偕逃至良乡 - Describes Xiulan's clever plan to escape with Huang by pretending to go to a temple fair.
  • 投宿一老媪家 - Indicates they sought refuge in the home of an elderly woman.
  • 叩媪姓氏，乃黄之从母，而刘女幼时呼为干阿奶者也 - Reveals that the elderly woman is Huang's aunt and was called 'gàn ānai' (a kind of godmother) by Xiangzhu when she was young.
  • 于是始知秀兰即香珠 - Concludes that Xiulan is indeed Xiangzhu.
  • 媪为买花烛，具鼓乐，而成夫妇 - Describes how the elderly woman arranged a wedding ceremony, and they became husband and wife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乡人 - Refers to local villagers or residents.
  • 脔割 - A brutal act of dismemberment, often associated with punishment or retribution.
  • 焚之 - Burning, which can symbolize finality or purification.
  • 臭闻里外 - The smell spreading throughout the neighborhood, indicating the severity of the act.
  • 《说文》 - The *Shuowen Jiezi*, an ancient Chinese dictionary that provides etymological and descriptive information about characters.
  • 螭 - A mythical creature resembling a dragon but with distinct characteristics.
  • 韦昭 - Wei Zhao, a scholar who provided annotations for the *Han Shu*.
  • 京师 - The capital city, in this case, Beijing.
  • 十刹海 - A famous area in Beijing known for its temples and scenic spots.
  • 烟袋斜街 - A street in Beijing known for its historical significance and cultural landmarks.
  • 山西 - Shanxi province, a region in northern China.
  • 刘姓 - The surname Liu, indicating a family name.
  • 小字 - A pet name or nickname.
  • 许嫁 - To be betrothed or promised in marriage.
  • 学贾 - To learn trade or business.
  • 中落 - To fall into poverty or decline.
  • 流落烟花 - To become involved in the entertainment industry, often implying a fall from grace.
  • 积累得数百金 - To accumulate a significant amount of wealth.
  • 挟之至都 - To bring wealth back to the capital.
  • 求故剑 - To seek out one's former betrothed.
  • 逆旅 - An inn or lodging place.
  • 利其腰缠 - To covet someone's wealth.
  • 诱与冶游 - To lure someone into a life of debauchery or promiscuity.
  • 堕其计 - To fall for a plan or scheme.
  • 极相得 - To be very close or compatible.
  • 无何 - Soon after or shortly thereafter.
  • 妓家 - A brothel or the household of a courtesan.
  • 逐客之令 - An order to expel guests or visitors.
  • 庙会 - A temple fair or religious festival.
  • 良乡 - A town near Beijing.
  • 老媪 - An elderly woman.
  • 从母 - Aunt or maternal aunt.
  • 干阿奶 - A term of endearment for a godmother or a close female relative.
  • 花烛 - Wedding candles, symbolizing the union of a couple.
  • 具鼓乐 - To prepare musical instruments and music for a celebration.

Proper Nouns

  • 乡人 - Local villagers or residents.
  • 《说文》 - The *Shuowen Jiezi* dictionary.
  • 韦昭 - Wei Zhao, a scholar who provided annotations for the *Han Shu*.
  • 京师 - The capital city, in this case, Beijing.
  • 十刹海 - A famous area in Beijing known for its temples and scenic spots.
  • 烟袋斜街 - A street in Beijing known for its historical significance and cultural landmarks.
  • 山西 - Shanxi province, a region in northern China.
  • 刘姓 - The surname Liu, indicating a family name.
  • 香珠 - A pet name or nickname for the girl.
  • 黄某 - A local man named Huang.
  • 秀兰 - A courtesan named Xiulan.
  • 良乡 - A town near Beijing.
  • 老媪 - An elderly woman.

[215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乱时为贼中大头目据为伪府 - Describes a chaotic period when a major bandit leader occupied a large mansion as a fake government office.
  • 每逢阴雨，辄见一女子形 - Describes a recurring phenomenon where a woman's image appears during rainy weather.
  • 洗之不去，天晴即没 - Describes the persistence of the image until the weather clears.
  • 遂扃闭不处 - Indicates that the house was sealed and not used.
  • 不意其今为祟也 - Expresses surprise that the woman's spirit is causing trouble now.
  • 此事吾知之 - Indicates that the speaker knows the truth about the incident.
  • 我陷贼中，即隶此贼帐下 - Describes the speaker's experience of being captured by the bandits and serving under the leader.
  • 贼获此女，欲污之，骂詈不从 - Describes the bandit leader's attempt to defile the woman and her refusal.
  • 杖数百，骂益厉，遂活埋之 - Describes the severe punishment the woman received and her ultimate fate.
  • 闻于官，官命焚之 - Describes the official's response to the discovery of the woman's body.
  • 此事吾知之 - Reiterates that the speaker knows the truth about the incident.
  • 惜叟知其事而不能举其姓氏 - Expresses regret that the old man knew the truth but could not provide the woman's name.
  • 不得为之表彰也 - Indicates that the woman could not be honored.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乱时 - Refers to a period of social unrest or chaos, which is a common theme in Chinese literature and history.
  • 贼中大头目 - Refers to a leading figure among bandits, which was a common social issue during times of political instability.
  • 伪府 - Refers to a counterfeit or false government office, which was often used by bandits to establish control over a region.
  • 扬州左卫街 - Refers to a specific location in Yangzhou, a city in China known for its historical significance and cultural heritage.
  • 扬州 - A city in China with a rich history and cultural significance, often mentioned in classical literature.
  • 左卫街 - A street in Yangzhou, which may have historical or cultural importance.
  • 亲串 - Refers to relatives or close friends, indicating a personal connection to the property.
  • 官 - Refers to the government or authorities, which played a significant role in maintaining order and justice.
  • 攻皮之工 - Refers to a person skilled in tanning or leatherworking, which was a common craft in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 扬州左卫街 - A specific location in Yangzhou, China.
  • 扬州 - A city in China with historical and cultural significance.
  • 左卫街 - A street in Yangzhou, which may have historical or cultural importance.
  • 官 - Refers to the government or authorities.
  • 攻皮之工 - Refers to a person skilled in tanning or leatherworking.

[216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 金陵自遭兵燹后 - Jinling (Nanjing) after suffering from war and destruction.
  • 往往于城中住屋内掘得棺木 - Often in the city's houses, they dug up coffins.
  • 盖皆乱中渴葬者也 - All were hastily buried in the chaos.
  • 以瓦砾堆积成山 - Piled up with tiles and rubble into a mountain.
  • 命兵勇平治之 - Ordered soldiers to level it.
  • 掘出棺二十余具 - Dug out over twenty coffins.
  • 发视之 - Opened them to see.
  • 一棺有女尸，周身衣服绚烂如新 - One coffin contained a female corpse, her clothes as bright and new as if freshly made.
  • 然皆已成灰，随手而碎 - But they were all turned to ash and crumbled at the touch.
  • 其尸未坏，肤肉白润，颊上粉泽犹存 - Her body was not decayed, her skin white and moist, with traces of makeup still on her cheeks.
  • 又一棺有男尸，坐而不卧 - Another coffin contained a male corpse, sitting upright rather than lying down.
  • 又一棺已成僵尸，上半身生兽毛，下半身生鸟羽，尤可怪也 - Another coffin contained a zombie, with animal fur on the upper body and bird feathers on the lower body, which is particularly strange.
  • 达官闻之，命将诸棺迁葬他处，不许开视 - The high-ranking official heard about it and ordered the coffins to be moved to another place, forbidding any further examination.
  • 黔中有任某者，续娶胡氏，再嫁妇也 - In Guizhou, there was a man named Ren who remarried Hu, a woman who had been widowed and remarried.
  • 然甚贤淑，抚前妻子如己出 - She was very virtuous and treated her husband's children as her own.
  • 数年后，任大病，胡侍奉汤药，支持门户，累月不懈 - After several years, Ren fell seriously ill, and Hu served him medicine and supported the household, tirelessly for months.
  • 见夫病笃，知不可为，乃先服毒，欲与俱死，抱夫痛哭，夫卒亦卒 - Seeing her husband's condition worsen and knowing he could not be saved, she first took poison, wanting to die with him. She hugged him and cried, and when he died, she did too.
  • 亲友入吊，咸叹其烈 - Friends and relatives came to mourn, all praising her loyalty.
  • 亦有议其不死前夫为可惜者 - Some also thought it was a pity that she did not die with her first husband.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金陵 - Refers to Nanjing, a city in Jiangsu Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 兵燹 - Refers to the destruction and chaos caused by war, often used to describe the aftermath of battles.
  • 王府园 - Refers to a garden belonging to a prince or noble, indicating a place of high status.
  • 黔中 - Refers to Guizhou Province, a region in southwestern China.

Proper Nouns

  • 金陵 - Nanjing, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 王府园 - A garden belonging to a prince or noble.
  • 黔中 - Guizhou Province, a region in southwestern China.
  • 兰陵公主 - Princess Lanling, a historical figure recorded in the *Book of Sui*.

[217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 京师西单牌楼 - Specific location in Beijing, Xidan Plaza.
  • 大宅 - Refers to a large house or mansion.
  • 狐居之 - Indicates that the house is inhabited by foxes.
  • 无赁者 - Means that no one is renting or living in the house.

  • 及期而往，则曰：“日已选矣，幸勿稍有更动。” - When Chen returns, Xia tells him that the day has already been selected and advises against any changes.

So he allowed him to manage himself, buying a few herbs each day to take, even though it was ineffective, it did no harm.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '忽見有婦人與同臥床中' is elliptical and could be interpreted as 'Suddenly saw a woman lying in bed with her' or 'Suddenly saw a woman lying in bed with her in the same room.'
  • The phrase '惟所語者讠連讠娄細碎' is elliptical and could be interpreted as 'Only what she said was incoherent and trivial' or 'Only the conversation was incoherent and trivial.'
  • The clause '江氏婦大戚，自知及期必不能免' is elliptical and could be interpreted as 'The Jiang family's daughter-in-law was greatly distressed, knowing that she would inevitably die upon giving birth' or 'The Jiang family's daughter-in-law was greatly distressed, knowing that she would inevitably die upon giving birth in childbirth.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This long sentence describes a series of events involving the moving statue and the subsequent actions taken to prevent it from moving again
  • This sentence introduces the new structure and its strange phenomena
  • This long sentence describes the encounter between Zhao Chang and the tall man
  • This sentence describes another person's encounter with the tall man and his determination to follow him
  • This sentence contrasts the fates of Zhao Chang and the other person
  • This sentence describes the common sightings of the tall man and the various names given to different types of mysterious figures
  • This sentence includes a quote from Feng Mengxiang, suggesting that the tall man might be a spirit related to an old tree in the south of the city

Proper Nouns

  • 冯氏 - A surname
  • 冯梦香孝廉 - A name
  • 山魈 - A mythical creature

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 同治乙丑春間 - Refers to the spring of 1865, using the traditional Chinese calendar system. '乙丑' is the stem-branch combination for the year 1865.
  • 嚴州城中 - Refers to the city of Yanzhou (modern-day Jiande, Zhejiang).
  • 夜夜聞鬼叫 - Literally 'hearing ghostly cries every night,' a common expression in Chinese literature to describe eerie or supernatural occurrences.
  • 烏烏哑哑 - Onomatopoeic terms for the sound of the cries, often used to describe the voices of ghosts or spirits.
  • 如小犬鳴 - A simile comparing the sound to that of a small dog barking.
  • 居東北者以為聲在西南 - A relative clause indicating that people living in the northeast believed the sound was coming from the southwest.
  • 忽遠忽近，聲無定在 - Describes the sound as fluctuating in distance and location, emphasizing its mysterious and unsettling nature.
  • 而終夜不絕 - Means 'and throughout the night without ceasing.'
  • 論者曰 - A phrase introducing a comment or opinion from observers.
  • 此必死粤寇之難者，魂无所归也 - A conclusion drawn by observers, suggesting that the cries were the souls of those who died in the Taiping Rebellion and had no place to rest.
  • 袁君之京字怀西者 - Refers to a Mr. Yuan who was in charge of education in Jiande County.
  • 方权建德教谕 - Indicates that he was temporarily in charge of the educational affairs in Jiande.
  • 乃出己赀为倡 - Means 'then he donated his own money to lead the effort.'
  • 醵钱于四城门各放焰口一坛 - Describes the act of collecting money to perform a ritual called 'fire mouth' (焰口) at each of the four city gates.
  • 并焚纸钱无算 - Means 'and burned an incalculable number of paper money.'
  • 果三夜无鬼声 - Means 'indeed, there were no ghostly cries for three nights.'
  • 其第四夜复然 - Means 'but on the fourth night, the cries returned.'
  • 协镇朱君仿而行之 - Refers to a Mr. Zhu, the assistant military governor, who followed the same practice.
  • 亦三夜无声 - Means 'there were also no cries for three nights.'
  • 吴和甫学使按试严州 - Refers to Mr. Wu, the provincial examiner, who was inspecting the examinations in Yanzhou.
  • 居考棚 - Means 'stayed in the examination hall.'
  • 以问众广文 - Means 'asked the scholars about the matter.'
  • 袁君以告 - Means 'Mr. Yuan informed him.'
  • 学使命各城门放焰口，如袁、朱二君故事 - Means 'the provincial examiner ordered the same ritual to be performed at each city gate, following the example of Mr. Yuan and Mr. Zhu.'
  • 又自为文祭之 - Means 'and he also wrote an article to offer a sacrifice.'
  • 自是鬼声遂绝 - Means 'from then on, the ghostly cries ceased.'

  • 广文 - A general term for scholars or literati.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The text is described as strange, vulgar, and lowbrow. It was printed using woodblock printing by a paper-selling shop and stamped with the seal of the Immortal Master Ge. Scholars often laughed at it, believing it was created by monks as a heretical doctrine for profit. Women often cried out to burn the documents when they were dying, and their eyes closed only after the document was burned. Sometimes the flames were seen to take the shape of intertwining dragons. Mr. Feng Mengxiang, a scholar-official, said that the way of heaven and earth is nothing but sincerity. Countless ordinary couples, with their sincere intentions, are firmly united. Who knows if the underworld has added such a rule? Everything, if not sincere, is false even if it is true; if sincere, it is true even if it is false. I think this argument is correct. The *Doctrine of the Mean* says, 'Without sincerity, there is nothing.' It can be known that sincerity is the very thing. Ancient ritual objects and modern paper money can all be understood in this way.

  • 冯梦香孝廉 - Mr. Feng Mengxiang, a scholar-official.
  • 《中庸》 - The *Doctrine of the Mean*.
  • 明器 - Ritual objects used in ancient funerals.
  • 纸钱 - Paper money burned as part of funeral rituals.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 居母所 - Reflects the cultural practice of a widow living with her mother, especially in traditional Chinese society.
  • 兄弟之阈 - Highlights the importance of family boundaries and respect for one's brothers' domains.

  • 中溜井灶之神 - These are traditional Chinese household deities associated with different aspects of daily life and the home.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The clause '惟居母所，虽兄弟之阈，不履也' is elliptical and must be read as 'She only stayed at her mother's place, even though she did not step over the threshold of her brothers' homes.'
  • The sequence of events in '至是乃遍诣其伯叔兄弟，留连数日而还' is compressed, so the sentence must be expanded to 'At this time, she visited all her uncles, aunts, and brothers, staying for several days and then returning.'
  • The question '叔氏问姑娘使得否' is elliptical and must be expanded to 'Uncle asked if the girl could use the red silk.'
  • The repetition of '使得' in '敬谢叔父，使得，使得' emphasizes the affirmative response, but it must be rendered clearly in English.
  • The sequence of events in '甫就缢，即见其舅姑至，又偕一少年至，曰：“此尔夫也。”' is rapid and must be broken down to 'As soon as she hanged herself, she saw her husband's parents come, followed by a young man, who said, 'This is your husband.''
  • The sequence of supernatural appearances in '俄而中溜井灶之神悉至，社公亦至，皆力阻之' is rapid and must be broken down to 'Soon, all the household gods of the hearth, well, and stove came, followed by the village deity, who all tried to stop her.'

  • 是日天适微雨，侍疾者偶言及之，其伯母曰：“吾宫中不雨也。” - On the day she died, there was a light rain, and someone mentioned it to her. She replied that it did not rain in her 'palace.' '宫中' refers to a place of residence, possibly a metaphorical or spiritual realm.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty (618–907 AD), a period of significant cultural and literary development in China.
  • 西勞氏 - A surname, indicating the family background of the woman.
  • 鼓脹 - A disease characterized by fluid accumulation in the abdomen, often associated with liver disease or heart failure. It was sometimes misinterpreted as a sign of pregnancy, especially in widows.
  • 守貞 - Refers to the practice of remaining chaste, particularly after the death of a fiancé or spouse, which was highly valued in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 長齋奉佛 - Indicates the practice of fasting and Buddhist worship, which was common among devout individuals in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 足不及閾外 - Suggests a strict adherence to the domestic sphere, which was typical for women of that time.
  • 親黨 - Refers to relatives and friends, indicating the social network around the woman.
  • 鬼附其身 - Refers to the belief in spirits possessing humans, a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 雉经 - Refers to the method of suicide by hanging, which was a form of self-inflicted death in ancient China.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 金陵小仓山后有大悲庵 - In the rear of Xiaocang Mountain in Jinling, there is a Great Compassion Temple. '金陵' (Jinling) is an ancient name for Nanjing, and '大悲庵' (Dabai Temple) is a Buddhist temple.
  • 乱后屋宇挠 - After the chaos, the buildings were in disarray. '挠' (nao2 zhuang4) is a colloquial term indicating disorder or ruin.
  • 惟前殿与后楼存 - Only the front hall and the rear pavilion remained. '惟' (wei2) emphasizes exclusivity, and '存' (cun2) means 'remain'.
  • 贫且孤 - Poor and alone. '贫' (pin2) means 'poor,' and '孤' (gu1) means 'alone' or 'orphaned'.
  • 馆其中 - Lodged there. '馆' (guan3) can mean 'to lodge' or 'to stay'.
  • 日则于殿上授徒 - During the day, he taught his students on the hall. '日则' (ri4 ze2) means 'during the day,' and '授徒' (shou4 tu2) means 'to teach students'.
  • 夜则宿于楼 - At night, he slept in the pavilion. '夜则' (ye4 ze2) means 'at night,' and '宿' (su4) means 'to sleep'.
  • 轮流为执爨 - Rotated to cook. '轮流' (lun2 liu2) means 'rotated,' and '执爨' (zhi2 cuan4) means 'to cook'.
  • 庵久无僧 - The temple had been without monks for a long time. '久' (jiu3) means 'long,' and '无僧' (wu2 seng1) means 'no monks'.
  • 楼左右皆山 - The mountains surrounded the pavilion on both sides. '左右' (zuo3 you3) means 'both sides'.
  • 山中固多荒冢 - There were many deserted graves in the mountains. '固' (gu4) means 'indeed' or 'certainly,' and '荒冢' (huang1 zhong3) means 'deserted graves'.
  • 每夕阳西下，暮色苍茫 - Every time the sun set in the west, the evening became dim and hazy. '每' (mei3) means 'every time,' and '苍茫' (cang1 mang2) means 'dim and hazy'.
  • 恒见白衣人往来松下 - He often saw a white-clad person moving among the pines. '恒' (heng2) means 'often,' and '往来' (lai2 wang2) means 'to move back and forth'.
  • 吴妄意白者金象 - Wu foolishly thought that the white-clad person was a gold statue. '妄意' (wang4 yi4) means 'foolishly thought,' and '金象' (jin1 xiang4) means 'gold statue'.
  • 疑有藏金 - Suspected that there was hidden gold. '疑' (yi2) means 'suspect,' and '藏金' (cang2 jin1) means 'hidden gold'.
  • 伺之 - Watched for it. '伺' (si4) means 'to watch for' or 'to wait for'.
  • 知自一棺中出 - Knew that it came out from a coffin. '知' (zhi1) means 'knew,' and '自' (zi4) means 'from'.
  • 因托故早散诸徒 - Thus, he pretended to have an excuse and dismissed his disciples early. '因' (yin1) means 'thus,' '托故' (tuo2 gu4) means 'pretended to have an excuse,' and '早散' (zao3 san4) means 'dismissed early'.
  • 袖斧而往 - With an axe in his sleeve, he went. '袖斧' (xiu4 fu3) means 'with an axe in his sleeve'.
  • 乃斧未及棺，而棺中白衣人已出 - But before he could reach the coffin, the white-clad person had already emerged from it. '乃' (nai4) means 'but,' and '及' (ji2) means 'to reach'.
  • 则僵尸也 - It was a zombie. '则' (ze2) means 'then' or 'it turned out to be,' and '僵尸' (jiang1 shi2) means 'zombie'.
  • 大惊却走 - He was greatly startled and retreated. '大惊' (da4 jing1) means 'greatly startled,' and '却走' (que3 zou3) means 'retreated'.
  • 尸从之 - The zombie followed him. '从之' (cong2 zhi1) means 'followed him'.
  • 吴意僵尸不能逾沟 - Wu thought that the zombie could not cross the ditch. '意' (yi4) means 'thought,' and '逾沟' (yu2 gou1) means 'cross the ditch'.
  • 走坎科处避之 - He ran to a place with ditches and pits to avoid it. '坎科' (kan3 ke1) means 'ditches and pits'.
  • 而尸逾坑越谷如履平地 - But the zombie crossed the ditches and valleys as if walking on level ground. '逾坑越谷' (yu2 keng1 yue4 gu3) means 'crossed the ditches and valleys,' and '如履平地' (ru2 lv3 ping2 di4) means 'as if walking on level ground'.
  • 吴窘甚 - Wu was very窘 (in a difficult situation). '窘' (jiong3) means 'in a difficult situation,' and '甚' (shen4) means 'very'.
  • 奔还，庵门未及掩 - He ran back, but the temple door was not yet closed. '奔还' (ben1 huan2) means 'ran back,' and '未及掩' (wei4 ji2 yan3) means 'not yet closed'.
  • 而尸已及 - But the zombie had already caught up. '已及' (yi3 ji2) means 'had already caught up'.
  • 吴急走，上楼而仆 - Wu hurriedly ran, climbed the pavilion, and fell. '急走' (ji2 zou3) means 'hurriedly ran,' and '仆' (pu2) means 'fell'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 明日诸徒咸集 - 明日 means 'the next day,' 诸徒 refers to 'various disciples' or 'followers,' and 咸集 means 'all gathered.' This phrase sets the scene for a gathering the next day.
  • 见一白衣人僵立于梯 - 白衣人 literally means 'a white-clad person,' often used to describe a ghost or an undead figure. 僵立 means 'stiffly standing,' and 于梯 means 'on the ladder.' This phrase describes a ghostly figure standing motionless on a ladder.
  • 以长竿缚帚扑之 - 以长竿 means 'with a long pole,' 缚帚 means 'tied with a broom,' and 扑之 means 'to hit it.' This phrase describes the method used to hit the ghost.
  • 面如生，惟毛毵毵长寸许 - 面如生 means 'the face looks alive,' and 惟毛毵毵长寸许 means 'only the hair is scattered and about an inch long.' This phrase describes the ghost's appearance.
  • 以称锤压其胸 - 以称锤 means 'with a balance weight,' and 压其胸 means 'to press on its chest.' This phrase describes the action of pressing on the ghost's chest.
  • 惧其复起也 - 惧 means 'fear,' and 复起 means 'to rise again.' This phrase explains the reason for pressing on the ghost's chest.
  • 登楼视吴，则仆于床前 - 登楼 means 'climbed the stairs,' 视吴 means 'to look at Wu,' and 仆于床前 means 'fell on the bed.' This phrase describes Wu's state after the incident.
  • 口吐白沫，胸间犹温 - 口吐白沫 means 'spitting white foam,' and 胸间犹温 means 'the chest is still warm.' This phrase describes Wu's physical condition.
  • 以姜汤灌之，始苏 - 以姜汤 means 'with ginger soup,' 灌之 means 'to pour it on,' and 始苏 means 'began to revive.' This phrase describes the method used to revive Wu.
  • 诘得其故 - 诘 means 'to interrogate,' and 得其故 means 'to find out the reason.' This phrase describes the process of finding out what happened.
  • 君真大幸也 - 君 means 'you,' 真 means 'really,' 大幸 means 'great luck,' and 也 is a particle indicating a statement. This phrase expresses relief.
  • 甲乃返其尸于棺 - 甲 refers to a person named Jia, 乃 means 'then,' 返 means 'to return,' 其尸 means 'his body,' and 于棺 means 'to the coffin.' This phrase describes Jia's action.
  • 聚薪蒸而燔之 - 聚薪 means 'gathered firewood,' 蒸 means 'steamed,' and 燔之 means 'burned it.' This phrase describes the method used to burn the body.
  • 逾数月，吴亦物故 - 逾数月 means 'after several months,' 吴 refers to Wu, 亦 means 'also,' and 物故 means 'passed away.' This phrase describes Wu's eventual death.
  • 或问甲 - 或 means 'someone,' and 问甲 means 'asked Jia.' This phrase sets up a dialogue.
  • 尔子何乃衣白 - 尔子 means 'your son,' 何乃 means 'why,' and 衣白 means 'wearing white.' This phrase asks why Jia's son was wearing white.
  • 吾夫妇年逾六十，止此一子 - 吾夫妇 means 'my husband and I,' 年逾六十 means 'over sixty years old,' and 止此一子 means 'only this one son.' This phrase explains Jia's situation.
  • 此子死，族中又无可嗣 - 此子 means 'this son,' 死 means 'died,' 族中 means 'in the clan,' 又 means 'also,' 无 means 'no,' and 可嗣 means 'to inherit.' This phrase explains the consequences of the son's death.
  • 念身后竟无成服者 - 念 means 'thinking,' 身后 means 'after death,' 竟 means 'eventually,' and 无成服者 means 'no one to wear mourning clothes.' This phrase expresses Jia's concern.
  • 故预以白衣敛之也 - 故 means 'therefore,' 预 means 'in advance,' 以白衣 means 'with white clothes,' and 敛之 means 'to dress him.' This phrase explains why Jia dressed his son in white.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 白衣人 - In Chinese folklore, a 'white-clad person' often refers to a ghost or an undead figure. This term is used to describe the ghostly figure encountered in the story.
  • 称锤 - A 'balance weight' is a heavy object used to weigh things. In this context, it is used to press on the ghost's chest to prevent it from rising again.
  • 姜汤 - Ginger soup is a traditional Chinese remedy used to treat various ailments, including colds and nausea. In this context, it is used to revive Wu.
  • 物故 - 'Passed away' is a euphemism for death, often used in formal or literary contexts.
  • 成服 - 'To wear mourning clothes' is a traditional Chinese practice of wearing specific clothing to show respect for the deceased. In this context, it refers to the lack of someone to perform this duty for Jia's son.
  • 白衣敛之 - Dressing someone in white before burial is a traditional practice in Chinese funerary customs, often associated with the deceased's status or the family's wishes.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其后贵定之民皆爱戴之 - Indicates that the people of Guiding later became very fond of and respected a certain individual.
  • 辄有数百人从之 - '辄' is an adverb meaning 'always' or 'every time,' indicating a habitual action.
  • 萼楼止之城外，而从入城者犹数十人也 - Describes how even when the person stopped outside the city, dozens still followed him into the city.
  • 噪于大府之门 - '噪' means 'to clamor' or 'to shout,' suggesting a loud protest or demand.
  • 无夺我好官 - A direct statement expressing the people's refusal to accept the removal of their beloved official.
  • 虽已擢郡守，而其在县也，仍以县令冠服视事 - Indicates that despite being promoted to a higher position, the person still conducted official business in the county using the attire and formalities of a county magistrate.
  • 不敢言己迁官也 - Shows the humility and modesty of the official, who did not openly acknowledge his promotion.
  • 当是时大府亦倚以为重云 - Suggests that at that time, the provincial authorities also relied heavily on him.
  • 萼楼慷慷有大志 - '慷慷' is an onomatopoeic expression, often used to describe someone who is energetic and full of spirit.
  • 善用兵 - Indicates skill in military affairs.
  • 虽死不敢辞 - A strong statement of loyalty and dedication, even to the point of risking one's life.
  • 余甚壮之 - '壮' here means 'to admire' or 'to respect.'
  • 年未四十而殁 - Indicates that the person died before reaching the age of forty.
  • 惜哉 - An expression of regret or sorrow.
  • 余本拟为作传记存集中 - '拟' means 'intended' or 'planned.'
  • 因循未果 - '因循' means 'to delay' or 'to procrastinate,' and '未果' means 'not achieved.'
  • 姑撮大凡，存之笔记 - '姑' means 'temporarily' or 'for now,' and '撮大凡' means 'to summarize the main points.'
  • 古来忠臣义士，恒藉稗官小说以传 - '恒藉' means 'often rely on,' and '稗官小说' refers to unofficial historical records or novels.
  • 不必碑版文章然后照耀四裔也 - '碑版文章' refers to inscriptions on stone tablets or formal articles, and '照耀四裔' means 'to shine far and wide.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 恍惚间 - Means 'in a daze' or 'in a dreamlike state,' describing a semi-conscious or hallucinatory experience.
  • 冥隶持牌至 - Literally 'a ghostly officer with a tablet arrives,' referring to an official from the underworld.
  • 提归安学生员孙某 - A formal title or notice, likely meaning 'Summon the student of the prefecture of Gu'an named Sun.'
  • 可不到乎 - A rhetorical question meaning 'Can I not avoid it?'
  • 阴司公事与阳世异 - Means 'the affairs of the underworld differ from those of the mortal world,' emphasizing the inescapable nature of fate in the afterlife.
  • 不能幸免也 - Means 'cannot be avoided,' reinforcing the inevitability of the summons.
  • 许以重贿 - Means 'offered a large bribe,' indicating an attempt to bribe the official.
  • 促之行 - Means 'urged him to go,' describing the official's action.
  • 引孙入 - Means 'led Sun inside,' describing the official's action.
  • 跪案前 - Means 'kneeling before the table,' describing the posture of submission.
  • 汝是孙某乎 - Means 'Are you Sun?'
  • 然 - A simple affirmative, equivalent to 'yes.'
  • 叩头诡称 - Means 'kneeling and falsely claiming,' describing Sun's attempt to deceive.
  • 有母年七十余 - Means 'I have a mother who is over seventy years old,' providing the excuse for the request.
  • 愿增寿一纪 - Means 'I wish to have her life extended by one cycle (12 years),' a traditional Chinese unit of time.
  • 生平无善行 - Means 'no good deeds in life,' describing Sun's moral record.
  • 但知放债取重利 - Means 'only knew to lend money at high interest,' describing Sun's occupation and character.
  • 不减算幸矣 - Means 'it was fortunate that no interest was deducted,' implying that Sun's luck was bad.
  • 取案头一册 - Means 'took a book from the table,' describing the official's action.
  • 以朱笔点之 - Means 'marked it with a red pen,' describing the official's action.
  • 投笔砰然 - Means 'threw the pen down with a bang,' describing the official's frustration.
  • 悸而觉 - Means 'frightened and woke up,' describing Sun's reaction.
  • 增寿一纪 - Means 'extend her life by one cycle (12 years),' repeating the request.
  • 其夜遂死 - Means 'that night he died,' indicating the outcome.
  • 神不以此斥其妄求 - Means 'the god did not rebuke him for his foolish request,' indicating the god's leniency.
  • 盖未检册，虽神亦不知也 - Means 'because the book was not checked, even the god did not know,' explaining why the request was not granted.
  • 有桑田数十亩 - Means 'has dozens of acres of mulberry fields,' describing Sun's property.
  • 蚕时以叶贷于人 - Means 'lent leaves to people during the silkworm season,' describing Sun's business practice.
  • 新丝出 - Means 'when the new silk was produced,' describing the timing of repayment.
  • 收叶钱 - Means 'collected the leaf money,' describing the repayment.
  • 必数倍于市价 - Means 'at several times the market price,' describing the high interest rate.
  • 神所云重利 - Means 'the god mentioned the high interest,' referring to the practice of lending at high rates.
  • 其即此欤 - Means 'is this what he meant?' expressing doubt or uncertainty.

  • 為患如故 - Describes the problem continuing as before.
  • 有士人語之曰 - Describes a scholar speaking to them.
  • 妖不勝德 - A classical saying meaning that evil cannot overcome virtue.
  • 子宜力行善事，庶足以禳之 - Advises doing good deeds to ward off the evil.
  • 設位以祀，適助其焰，無益也 - Describes how setting up an altar to worship the spirits actually helped fuel the fires.
  • 主人從其言，自矢於神，力行善事，撤所設位，而火患良已 - Describes the owner following the advice, vowing to the gods, doing good deeds, removing the altar, and finally, the fires stopping.

  • 始知两兄惊仆以此也 - Means 'they finally realized that their two brothers had fainted because of this,' with '始知' meaning 'finally realized' and '以此也' meaning 'because of this.'
  • 甲、乙乃苏 - Means 'A and B then woke up,' with '乃苏' meaning 'then woke up.'
  • 亟捩舵而还 - Means 'quickly turned the rudder and returned,' with '亟' meaning 'quickly' and '捩舵' meaning 'turn the rudder.'
  • 不复操此业 - Means 'no longer engaged in this business,' with '不复' meaning 'no longer' and '操此业' meaning 'engaged in this business.'
  • 或曰：丙不去，则甲、乙死生未可知 - Means 'someone said: If C had not left, it would have been uncertain whether A and B would live or die,' with '或曰' meaning 'someone said' and '未可知' meaning 'uncertain.'
  • 丙之去，殆有使之者乎？ - Means 'C's departure, was it perhaps caused by someone?,' with '殆' meaning 'perhaps' and '使之者' meaning 'someone who caused it.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '及行至半途，乙语甲曰：“今日弟不来，差安静矣。”' introduces a situation and a conversation, with '及行至半途' setting the scene and '乙语甲曰' introducing the dialogue.
  • The sentence '丙突出曰：“我在此！”' introduces a sudden appearance and a statement, with '丙突出' indicating a sudden action and '曰' introducing the statement.
  • The sentence '旋至一处泊舟，甲将登岸，甫出舟，复跌入舟中，乙继出亦然。' describes a sequence of events, with '旋至一处泊舟' setting the scene, '甲将登岸' introducing the first action, and '甫出舟，复跌入舟中，乙继出亦然' describing the repeated action.
  • The sentence '丙不解其故，试出觇之，则一巨蛇，粗若木桶，其色纯黑，蟠卧沙滩上，闻人声，若惊觉者，仰首向天，张口嘘气。' describes a detailed observation, with '丙不解其故' introducing the lack of understanding, '试出觇之' describing the action of looking out, and the subsequent clauses detailing the appearance and behavior of the snake.
  • The sentence '俄见蛇身徐引而直，蜿蜒自去，在风声从之。其行甚速，顷刻遂杳。' describes the snake's movement, with '俄见蛇身徐引而直' describing the initial movement and '蜿蜒自去，在风声从之' describing the snake's departure.
  • The sentence '始知两兄惊仆以此也，遂呼曰：“蛇去矣！”甲、乙乃苏，亟捩舵而还，不复操此业。' describes the realization and subsequent actions, with '始知两兄惊仆以此也' introducing the realization, '遂呼曰' describing the shout, and '甲、乙乃苏，亟捩舵而还，不复操此业' describing the actions taken.
  • The sentence '或曰：丙不去，则甲、乙死生未可知，丙之去，殆有使之者乎？' introduces a hypothetical scenario and a question, with '或曰' introducing the statement and '丙之去，殆有使之者乎' posing the question.

  • 次子登甫孝廉世培时年甫十二 - His second son, Dengfu, who was a scholar-official named Shipai, was only twelve years old.   • 贼以其年幼力弱，弃之于途 - The rebels, thinking him too young and weak, abandoned him on the road. '贼' (Zéi) means 'rebels,' '以' (Yǐ) means 'because,' '其' (Qí) means 'he,' '年幼力弱' (Nián yòu lì ruò) means 'too young and weak,' '弃之于途' (Qì zhī yú tú) means 'abandoned him on the road.'

  • 若有人推其背曰 - As if someone pushed his back and said. '若' (Ruò) means 'as if,' '有人' (Yǒu rén) means 'someone,' '推其背' (Tuī qí bèi) means 'pushed his back,' '曰' (Yuē) means 'said.'

  • 其前隐隐有灯光 - In front of him, there was a faint light. '其前' (Qí qián) means 'in front of him,' '隐隐' (Yǐn yǐn) means 'faint,' '有灯光' (Yǒu dēng guāng) means 'there was a light.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '张知有异，以屋券示之曰' must be read as Zhang showing the lease agreement to his wife and speaking to her.
  • The clause '屋虽汝赁，然其下我藏有金银' indicates that even though the house is rented by Zhang, there are hidden treasures below.
  • The clause '或盗我财，奈何' suggests a concern about theft and a question about what to do about it.
  • The clause '尔附我妻而言，必为泉下人矣' implies that the wife is speaking through a ghost.
  • The clause '金银皆人间所用之物，尔无用焉，苦守奚为' argues that the treasures are useless to the ghost and should not be kept.
  • The clause '不如指以示我，我得尔财，为尔广作佛事' suggests a deal where Zhang gets the treasures in exchange for performing Buddhist rituals for the ghost.
  • The clause '彼此均有利益' concludes that both parties will benefit from the arrangement.

  • 杭音 - The dialect or accent of Hangzhou.
  • 泉下人 - A term for ghosts or spirits in the afterlife.
  • 佛事 - Buddhist rituals or ceremonies.

  • 忽雷雨骤至 - '忽' means 'suddenly,' '雷雨' means 'thunderstorm,' '骤至' means 'came suddenly.'

  • 又数人以网蒙其父头 - '又数人' means 'several more people,' '以网蒙' means 'covered with a net,' '其父头' means 'his father's head.'
  • 炬火即从之下击 - '炬火' means 'torch,' '即从之下击' means 'immediately struck down from below.'
  • 辟历一声 - '辟历' is an archaic term for 'thunder.'
  • 其子惊仆 - '惊仆' means 'fainted in shock.'
  • 起视 - '起视' means 'got up and looked.'
  • 则其父死矣 - '则' means 'then,' '其父死矣' means 'his father was dead.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 青衣者 - '青衣' can refer to officials or servants dressed in green, often associated with lower-ranking officials or attendants.
  • 辟历 - An archaic term for thunder, often used in Classical Chinese to add a sense of awe or supernatural significance.
  • 云气 - Clouds were often seen as omens or manifestations of divine intervention in ancient Chinese beliefs.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This clause is elliptical and can be read as 'once by disputing over land, he caused someone's death.'
  • This clause is a simple statement but can be expanded to 'Suddenly, a thunderstorm came.'
  • This clause can be read as 'saw a noisy crowd of people and horses in the air, about several dozen people.'
  • This clause is parallel and can be read as 'some were holding torches in their hands, others were carrying an object like a fishing net.'
  • This clause can be read as 'two people dressed in green took away the rice his father had harvested.'
  • This clause can be read as 'several more people covered his father's head with a net, and the torch immediately struck down from below.'
  • This clause can be read as 'a thunderclap, and his son fainted in shock.'
  • This clause can be read as 'got up and looked, then his father was dead.'
  • This clause can be read as 'the people he had seen before, all turned into clouds and soared into the sky and left.'

  • 夜闭之笼中，而置诸庭 - At night, closed in a cage, and placed in the courtyard, indicating the care taken for the cricket.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 长官命传诸讼者 - Formal expression meaning 'the magistrate ordered the summoning of all litigants.'
  • 焦头烂额者无虑数千人 - Vivid idiom meaning 'those with burnt heads and melted faces, numbering in the thousands,' likely referring to victims of a disaster or conflict.
  • 环呼索命 - Means 'to cry out for vengeance in a circle,' indicating a collective demand for justice or retribution.
  • 婉言谕之 - Means 'to gently advise or persuade them,' emphasizing the magistrate's diplomatic approach.
  • 童子无知 - Means 'the child was unaware or ignorant,' referring to the innocence or lack of understanding of the child involved.
  • 罔识轻重 - Means 'failed to recognize the gravity of the situation,' indicating a lack of judgment.
  • 降其科名 - Means 'to reduce his rank or title,' referring to the punishment of the child.
  • 减其禄算 - Means 'to decrease his lifespan or fortune,' indicating a reduction in his life expectancy or luck.
  • 美中不足 - Means 'imperfections in perfection,' referring to the idea that even in success, there are flaws.
  • 幺麽微物 - Means 'tiny and insignificant creatures,' referring to the insects in a derogatory manner.
  • 贪慕腥膻 - Means 'greedily craving for animal flesh,' describing the insects' desire for food.
  • 污生人之厨灶 - Means 'to defile the kitchens of the living,' referring to the insects' intrusion into human spaces.
  • 侵死者之墓隧 - Means 'to intrude into the tombs of the dead,' indicating the insects' presence in burial grounds.
  • 喧哗 - Means 'to make a loud noise or clamor,' describing the insects' collective protest.
  • 殆将用武 - Means 'nearly ready to resort to violence,' indicating the insects' threat of physical confrontation.
  • 膺冥罚 - Means 'to receive divine punishment,' referring to the child's punishment as a form of divine retribution.
  • 蔽辜 - Means 'to cover or atone for guilt,' suggesting that the punishment is sufficient to absolve the child of blame.
  • 苏 - Means 'to revive or awaken,' referring to the protagonist's return to consciousness.
  • 甫入 - Means 'as soon as entering,' indicating the immediate timing of the encounter.
  • 不交一言 - Means 'without exchanging a word,' describing the lack of communication between the protagonist and the previous guest.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长官 - Refers to a magistrate or local official, a key figure in traditional Chinese governance.
  • 讼者 - Refers to litigants or those involved in legal disputes, a common theme in Chinese legal and moral narratives.
  • 冥罚 - Refers to divine punishment, a concept rooted in Chinese religious and philosophical traditions.
  • 汪某 - Likely a proper name, possibly referring to the child or another figure in the narrative.
  • 将军 - Refers to a military leader, a figure of authority in Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以客言告 - Told him what the guest had said. '以...告' means to inform or tell.
  • 缕述前事 - Described the events in detail. '缕述' means to recount in detail.

  • 生还之望 - Hope of returning to life. '生还' means to return to life.
  • 裒然居首 - Stood out as the first. '裒然' means to stand out or be prominent.

  • 宣庙 - The temple of the emperor, a respectful term for the emperor's temple.
  • 悼惜 - Mourned and lamented, expressing deep sorrow and regret.
  • 赠太子太保 - Posthumously granted the title of Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince, a high honor.
  • 赐祭葬 - Bestowed a funeral sacrifice and burial, a special honor for high-ranking officials.
  • 故事 - According to custom, referring to traditional practices.

  • 得予谥 - Is eligible for a posthumous title. '谥' means posthumous title.
  • 礼臣以请 - The minister of rites requested it. '礼臣' means the minister of rites.
  • 会畿辅旱 - When the capital region suffered from drought. '畿辅' means the capital region, and '旱' means drought.
  • 宵旰忧勤 - Worked tirelessly day and night. '宵旰' means from dawn to dusk, and '忧勤' means to work hard.

  • 卒不得谥 - Ultimately did not receive a posthumous title. '卒' means ultimately, and '不得谥' means did not receive a posthumous title.
  • 殆即所谓降科名，减禄算，美中不足者欤 - Perhaps this is what is meant by being demoted in rank, reduced in salary, and having some imperfections in perfection. '殆' means perhaps, '降科名' means being demoted in rank, '减禄算' means reduced in salary, and '美中不足' means having some imperfections in perfection.
  • 戕群蚁 - Killed a swarm of ants. '戕' means to kill, and '群蚁' means a swarm of ants.
  • 罹冥谴 - Suffered punishment in the afterlife. '罹' means to suffer, and '冥谴' means punishment in the afterlife.
  • 物命至重 - The value of life is extremely important. '物命' means the value of life, and '至重' means extremely important.
  • 可不慎欤 - How can one be careless? '可不慎' means how can one be careless, and '欤' is an interrogative particle.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 道光間 - The Daoguang period (1821-1850) of the Qing dynasty, a time of internal strife and external pressures.
  • 某觀察者 - A title for a local official, such as a magistrate or prefect.
  • 性廉靜 - Describes the official as having a nature that is clean and quiet.
  • 公事之暇，辄至斋中读书 - In his spare time from official duties, he would go to his study to read.
  • 丙夜始就内寝 - The third watch of the night, around midnight; he would then go to bed.
  • 日以為常 - He did this every day.
  • 會屆中秋節 - Just as or coinciding with the Mid-Autumn Festival.
  • 制府某公素貪黩 - The governor-general, usually corrupt.
  • 饋獻不滿意，輒中傷之 - Gifts or offerings, unsatisfactory; to slander or harm him.
  • 觀察不得已，枉法得白金五百兩置書箧中 - The official had no choice; to pervert the law, five hundred taels of silver, put it in his book chest.
  • 以備書函並字之需 - To prepare for letters or correspondence and writing materials.
  • 時秋高夜靜，月色皎潔 - A clear and bright scene on a high autumn night.
  • 方執卷咿唔 - Holding a book, mumbling or reading aloud.
  • 忽飄風入户，為之栗然 - Suddenly, a gust of wind; shivering with fear.
  • 突見一女子戴黑纱幞头，衣粉紅绡衣，近案而立 - Wearing a black silk headscarf, wearing a pink silk robe, standing near the desk.
  • 正驚顧間，其後又立一丈夫，赤發深目，須如猬毛 - While he was still startled and looking around; red hair and deep-set eyes, beard like a hedgehog's quills.
  • 直前謂觀察曰：“公所得金盍以為長者壽？” - Approached the official and said; the gold you have obtained; why not use it as a gift for the elderly?
  • 觀察未之應 - Did not respond.
  • 而女子袖中出白丸摩弄之，光灼烁如一匹練 - Took out a white ball from her sleeve, played with it, shone brightly like a silken ribbon.
  • 觀察惧，發箧出金陳案上，女子盡納之袖 - The official was afraid, opened the chest and took out the gold, placed it on the desk, the woman put all of it in her sleeve.
  • 丈夫又謂觀察曰：“以公平時固廉吏，故不相害。然此不義物，非公所宜有，謹將去，成公之廉。” - Since you have always been a clean official, therefore we have not harmed you; but this unjust gain, is not something you should have, I will take it away, to maintain your integrity.
  • 言已，與女子俱出，倏忽不見 - After saying this, they both left, disappeared suddenly.
  • 殆古劍侠之流欤？ - Probably an ancient swordswoman or swordsman.
  • 觀察秘其事，旋移疾歸 - Kept the matter secret, soon pretended to be ill and returned home.
  • 既歸里，始與所親者言之 - After returning home, he then told those close to him about it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions
